frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre bhai, bura toh lagta hai sunke, but practical baat karo toh aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai. Ladki ko bhi samajhna chahiye ki itni raat ko akeli ghoomna, party karke nikalna safe nahi hota. Hum log blame nahi kar rahe, par apna khayal toh khud hi rakhna padta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and shifts attention away from male violence. It uses protective, practical-sounding language to justify restrictions on women's movement at night.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
